state.text: ║     HOW TO BUILD AN 8×8 PIXEL SCREEN FROM LOGIC GATES           ║
3  SWITCH   row address  RA0=bit1  RA1=bit2  RA2=bit4
1  BUTTON   WRITE  (hold to latch the row)
11  NOT      one per switch  (3 for RA, 8 for D)
16  AND      3-to-8 row decoder  (2 AND gates per row)
Goal: from RA2 RA1 RA0 (binary) activate exactly one of 8 row lines.
For each row r, build two AND gates in series:
stage1 = AND( RA0 or !RA0 ,  RA1 or !RA1 )   ← pick raw or inverted
stage2 = AND( stage1,          RA2 or !RA2 )  ← this is sel[r]
Use the raw switch if that bit is 1 in r, or the NOT if it is 0:
Build one of these per pixel (64 total).  r=row 0-7,  c=col 0-7:
selD  = AND( sel[r],  D[c]  )   ← 'this pixel should turn ON'
setG  = AND( selD,    WRITE )   ← only act during a WRITE
selND = AND( sel[r],  !D[c] )   ← 'this pixel should turn OFF'
rstG  = AND( selND,   WRITE )   ← only act during a WRITE
notQ  = NOT( DELAY output )     ← complement of stored value
holdG = AND( notQ,    rstG  )   ← reset/hold path
store = OR ( setG,    holdG )   → feed into DELAY
DELAY output → LED              ← the visible pixel
DELAY output → NOT(Q) (feedback loop closes the latch)
· All cells in row r share the same sel[r] and WRITE signals.
· All cells in col c share the same D[c] and !D[c] signals.
· Build decoder rows top-to-bottom in the LOGIC section below.
· Repeat the pixel cell for each of the 64 (row, col) positions.
For each row: set RA switches → set D switches → hold WRITE → release.
Row  RA2 RA1 RA0   D7 D6 D5 D4 D3 D2 D1 D0   pixel pattern
TIP: write rows from R7 down to R0 to watch the face build up.
LEDs show the stored image.  Load a row then use controls below.
STEP 1 — ROW ADDRESS  (binary 3-bit, selects one of 8 rows)
STEP 2 — PIXEL DATA  (1 bit per column)
+ 64 PIXEL CELLS each: 6 AND · 1 NOT · 1 OR · 1 DELAY · 1 LED
